articles.title: Israel Says It Has Struck Iran As It Takes Missile Fire
articles.content:
迪拜10月27日电（美联社）——以色列和伊朗周一凌晨相互发动报复性打击，中东面临重新陷入全面地区战争的危险。也门胡塞武装当天也向以色列发射导弹，并警告将把以色列相关船只列为红海攻击目标，局势进一步升级。

以色列周一凌晨对伊朗中部和西部发动空袭，作为对德黑兰导弹袭击的回应，伊朗则以多轮攻击进行反击。这是4月8日停火协议达成以来的首次交火。以色列中部地区可听到爆炸声，防空系统试图拦截伊朗来袭火力。邻国约旦也响起防空警报。

伊朗警告称，美国将对任何局势升级负责。

伊朗外交部发言人埃斯梅尔·巴格艾在德黑兰举行的记者会上表示：“没有人相信以色列政权会在没有与美国协调的情况下采取任何行动。美国对以色列政权的侵略行为负有责任，任何紧张局势升级的后果也将由美国负责。”

伊朗称其目标是以色列军事基地

伊朗准军事组织伊斯兰革命卫队表示，已瞄准以色列两处军事基地，称此次袭击是“胜利行动”的一部分。革命卫队称，在以色列瞄准伊朗三个地区的雷达站后发射了导弹。以色列军方表示，其目标是基于卡车的地对空导弹发射装置。

周日，在以色列毫无预警地袭击贝鲁特南部郊区后，伊朗发出报复警告，此举公然违抗了华盛顿几天前的停火请求。

周一标志着伊朗战争的第100天。这场战争于2月28日爆发，当时以色列和美国击毙了最高领袖哈梅内伊及其他伊朗高级领导人。

战争持续至4月8日伊朗与美国达成停火协议，但实现永久停火的努力面临诸多挑战，包括伊朗对霍尔木兹海峡的控制——和平时期全球五分之一的贸易石油和天然气曾经过该海峡，以及以色列与黎巴嫩真主党之间的持续战斗。

随着全球能源供应受到威胁、伊朗仍持有大量高浓缩铀库存、也门胡塞武装周一加入战斗，全面战争再次爆发的风险似乎正在上升。

外交官争相挽救停火协议

两名地区官员表示，周一各国正在开展协调外交努力，以挽救伊朗与美国之间的停火协议，此前以色列和伊朗之间发生交火。

埃及、沙特阿拉伯、土耳其、巴基斯坦和卡塔尔的官员敦促美国政府向以色列施压，遏制其对伊朗和贝鲁特的袭击。他们还敦促伊朗官员停止对以色列的攻击。这两名官员因未被授权对媒体发言而要求匿名。

其中一名参与伊朗与美国之间调解工作的官员表示，巴基斯坦主导的调解人对以色列袭击贝鲁特南部郊区感到愤怒——当时巴基斯坦内政部长正在德黑兰进行新一轮推动美伊谈判的努力。

调解人告诉美国政府，以色列对贝鲁特的袭击“意在破坏我们达成协议的努力”，“特朗普必须阻止内塔尼亚胡的鲁莽行动”。

特朗普称“拍板的是我”，而非以色列

白宫未回应有关以色列空袭以及是否与美国协调的询问。

一名美国高级官员周日表示，在以色列对伊朗发动空袭前，美国总统特朗普曾致电以色列总理内塔尼亚胡，敦促他不要立即对伊朗导弹袭击进行报复。这名因讨论私人通话而要求匿名的官员表示，特朗普认为自己已说服内塔尼亚胡等待。

这位官员说：“特朗普让内塔尼亚胡暂时按兵不动。”但他拒绝透露通话的其他细节，内塔尼亚胡办公室也未立即置评。

特朗普早些时候告诉福克斯新闻频道记者，他希望伊朗人停止发射导弹并重返谈判桌。他还表示，以色列周日对黎巴嫩的袭击“与美国没有协调”，“我对此不满意”。

在以色列对伊朗发动空袭前，特朗普接受英国《金融时报》采访时坚称，他向内塔尼亚胡下达了战争如何进行的条款。

他在电话采访中说：“他没有选择余地。我来拍板。我决定一切。他（内塔尼亚胡）说了不算。”

胡塞武装声称袭击以色列

伊朗支持的也门胡塞武装周一声称对以色列发动了袭击，并表示以色列相关船只将成为红海攻击目标，使红海、亚丁湾和连接两者的曼德海峡面临危险。

胡塞武装发言人叶海亚·萨雷准将的声明在胡塞武装的马斯雅卫星电视台播出。在加沙地带的以色列-哈马斯战争期间，胡塞武装发动了100多次袭击，至少杀害了9名船员并击沉了4艘船只，袭击目标往往是与以色列有边缘关联或毫无关联的船只。

这些袭击颠覆了红海航运，战前每年约有1万亿美元货物通过该海域。

埃及苏伊士运河的通航也受到严重干扰，该运河连接红海和地中海。运河仍是埃及主要的外汇来源之一，2023年为其带来100亿美元收入，而埃及整体经济正面临困境。

胡塞武装的威胁升级之际，沙特阿拉伯正依靠其东西向输油管道通过红海出口石油，作为霍尔木兹海峡的替代路线。

以色列空袭伊朗

伊朗国家电视台报道称，在伊斯法罕、卡拉季、大不里士和德黑兰都可听到爆炸声，但未立即详细说明。德黑兰一名目击者描述称，在首都西部听到至少一次大爆炸。以色列袭击后，伊朗关闭了德黑兰主要机场霍梅尼国际机场的周边空域。

官员未提供被袭击目标的详情或损失信息。伊朗伊斯兰革命卫队表示，以色列在周一上午的袭击中使用了空射弹道导弹，但未详述。

半官方的法尔斯通讯社和梅赫尔通讯社称，以色列空袭击中了胡齐斯坦省马赫沙尔市的一家石化工厂，但未说明损失情况。以色列军方随后证实了对该石化工厂的袭击。

——

美联社记者马修·李（华盛顿）、米歇尔·L·普赖斯（新泽西州布里奇沃特）、埃莱娜·贝卡托罗斯（希腊雅典）、萨米·马格迪（开罗）对此报道亦有贡献。
articles.summary:
LOADINGERROR LOADING
&

DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Israel and Iran traded fire early Monday in retaliatory strikes that threatened to drag the Middle East back into a full-scale regional war, while Yemen’s Houthi rebels also fired at Israel and warned they would target Israel-affiliated ships in the Red Sea, further escalating tension.

Israel launched strikes on central and western Iran early Monday in response to missile fire from Tehran, and Iran retaliated with waves of attacks. It was the first exchange of fire since an April 8 ceasefire was reached. Explosions could be heard in central Israel as air defenses sought to intercept incoming Iranian fire. Sirens also sounded across neighboring Jordan.

Advertisement

Iran warned that the United States would be responsible for any escalation.

“No one believes that the Israeli regime would take any action without coordination with the United States,” Iranian Foreign Ministry spokesman Esmail Baghaei said during a briefing with journalists in Tehran. “The United States bears responsibility for the Israeli regime’s aggression, and it will also be responsible for the consequences of any escalation in tensions.”

Iran Says It Targeted Military Bases In Israel

Iran’s paramilitary Revolutionary Guard said it had targeted two military bases in Israel, describing the attacks as being part of Operation Nasr, or “Victory.” The Guard said it launched the missiles after Israel targeted radar sites in three areas of Iran. Israel’s military said it targeted truck-based surface-to-air missile launchers.

Tehran warned of retaliation on Sunday after Israel struck Beirut’s southern suburbs without warning in defiance of Washington’s request days ago to stand down.

Advertisement

Monday marked the 100th day of the Iran war, launched Feb. 28 when Israel and the United States killed Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei and other senior Iranian leaders.

The war raged until Iran and the U.S. reached a ceasefire on April 8, but efforts at a permanent end to the hostilities have been challenged by Iran’s chokehold on the Strait of Hormuz, through which a fifth of all traded oil and natural gas once passed in peacetime, as well as fighting between Israel and the Lebanese Shiite militia Hezbollah.

Advertisement

With global energy supplies threatened, Iran still holding a vast stockpile of highly enriched uranium and Yemen’s Houthi rebels getting involved in the fighting Monday, the risk of the war fully erupting again appears to be rising.

Diplomats Race To Save Ceasefire

Two regional officials said concerted diplomatic efforts were underway Monday to salvage the ceasefire between Iran and the United States after the exchange of strikes between Israel and Iran.

Officials from Egypt, Saudi Arabia, Turkey, Pakistan and Qatar, have urged the U.S. administration to pressure Israel to rein in its strikes on Iran and Beirut. They have also urged Iranian officials to stop attacks on Israel, they said. Both officials spoke on condition of anonymity because they were not authorized to speak to reporters.

Advertisement

One of the officials, who is involved in mediation efforts between Iran and the U.S., said the Pakistan-led mediators were furious about the Israeli strike on Beirut’s southern suburbs, which came while Pakistan’s interior minister was in Tehran in a fresh bid to push U.S.-Iranian negotiations forward.

The mediators told the U.S. administration that the Israeli strike on Beirut meant “to disrupt our efforts to reach a deal” and that “Trump has to stop Netanyahu’s reckless maneuvers.”

Trump Says ‘I Call The Shots,’ Not Israel

The White House did not respond to messages about the Israeli strikes and whether they were done in coordination with the U.S.

Advertisement

Speaking before Israel’s strikes on Iran, a senior U.S. official on Sunday said U.S. President Donald Trump had called Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu to urge him not to retaliate immediately for the Iranian missile attack. The official, who spoke on condition of anonymity to describe a private phone call, said Trump believed he had convinced Netanyahu to wait.

Trump “got Bibi to hold off for the time being,” the official said. The official would not offer any other details of the call, and there was no immediate comment from Netanyahu’s office.

Advertisement

Trump earlier told a Fox News Channel reporter he wanted the Iranians to stop firing missiles and return to the negotiating table. He also said Israel’s strikes in Lebanon earlier Sunday were not coordinated with the U.S. and “I’m not happy about it.”

Speaking to The Financial Times before the Israeli strikes on Iran, Trump insisted he dictated terms to Netanyahu on how the war should be prosecuted.

“He won’t have any choice,” Trump told the newspaper in a telephone interview. “I call the shots. I call all the shots. He (Netanyahu) doesn’t call the shots.”

Advertisement

Houthis Claim Attack On Israel

Yemen’s Iran-backed Houthi rebels on Monday claimed an attack on Israel and said Israel-affiliated vessels would again be a target in the Red Sea, putting the Red Sea, the Gulf of Aden and the narrow Bab el-Mandeb Strait connected them in danger.

The statement from Brig. Gen. Yahya Saree was broadcast on the Houthis’ al-Masirah satellite news channel. During the Israel-Hamas war in the Gaza Strip, the Houthis killed at least nine mariners and sank four ships in over 100 attacks, often targeting vessels with tangential or no ties to Israel.

The assaults upended shipping in the Red Sea, through which about $1 trillion of goods passed each year before the war.

Advertisement

They also greatly disrupted transits through Egypt’s Suez Canal, which links the Red Sea to the Mediterranean. The canal remains one of the top providers of hard currency for Egypt, providing it $10 billion in 2023 as its wider economy struggles.

The Houthis’ renewed threat also comes as Saudi Arabia is relying on its East-West Pipeline to export oil out through the Red Sea as an alternative to the Strait of Hormuz.

Israel Strikes Iran

Iranian state television reported the sound of explosions being heard in Isfahan, Karaj, Tabriz and Tehran, without immediately elaborating. A witness in Tehran described hearing at least one large blast to the west of the country’s capital. Iran closed the airspace around Tehran’s Imam Khomeini International Airport, the country’s main airfield, after the Israeli attack.

Advertisement

Officials offered no details on what had been struck, nor any damage information. Iran’s paramilitary Revolutionary Guard said Israel used air-launched ballistic missiles in its attack Monday morning, without elaborating.

The semiofficial Fars and Mehr news agencies said Israeli strikes had hit a petrochemical factory in city of Mahshahr in Khuzestan province. It did not elaborate on damage. The Israeli military later confirmed the strike on the petrochemical plant.

___

Associated Press writers Matthew Lee in Washington; Michelle L. Price in Bridgewater, New Jersey; Elena Becatoros in Athens, Greece; and Samy Magdy in Cairo contributed to this report.

Advertisement

Related

Israel

Iran

Iran war

Trump Reportedly Demands Israel Hold Back Revenge Amid Iran's 'Warning' Strikes For Beirut
Israel Says Iran Launched Missiles At It In The First Such Bombardment During Fragile Ceasefire
Israeli Airstrikes Kill 9 Including Lebanese Army Officers After Ceasefire Deal

articles.title: U.S. Considers Buying Chagos Islands, Telegraph Reports
articles.content:
6月7日（路透社电）——《每日电讯报》周日报道，白宫正在考虑从毛里求斯手中购买查戈斯群岛。

报道称，美国官员已起草一份提案，绕过英国，自行达成协议以取得迪戈加西亚岛的控制权。

报道指出，该计划是白宫正在起草的多个选项之一，旨在为英国首相基尔·斯塔默放弃印度洋群岛主权给毛里求斯提供替代方案。

一位美国官员告诉路透社：“特朗普总统一直坚持这一立场，即英国不应放弃英属印度洋领地，其中包括我们在迪戈加西亚环礁上与英国共建的军事设施。”

该官员称：“迪戈加西亚在印度洋的战略位置使其成为一个至关重要、不可或缺的军事基地，对美国的国家安全具有重大意义。”他补充说，美国仍在与英国定期讨论，以保持该岛作为地区安全平台的可行性。

英国政府4月暂停了将美英迪戈加西亚空军基地所在地查戈斯群岛主权移交给毛里求斯的协议，该协议曾遭到美国总统特朗普的批评。

特朗普2月称该协议是一个“重大错误”。

一位英国政府发言人表示：“政府接手了一个英国对迪戈加西亚军事基地的控制权受到威胁的局面，需要采取行动保护英国国家利益，防止对手在这个具有重要战略意义的地点获得立足点。”

该发言人补充道：“迪戈加西亚是英国和美国共同的关键战略军事资产，近60年来一直保护着我们的共同安全。维护迪戈加西亚的长期运营控制和安全，正是英毛协议的整个基础，这一协议源于两国都非常清楚的对该基地的真正长期风险。”

（由班加罗尔的格纳内什瓦尔·拉詹和瑞亚·罗斯·亚伯拉罕以及华盛顿的博·埃里克森报道；克里斯·里斯编辑）
articles.summary:
June 7 (Reuters) - The White House is considering a plan to buy the Chagos Islands from Mauritius, the Telegraph reported on Sunday.

U.S. officials have drawn up a proposal to bypass the U.K. and make their own deal to take control of Diego Garcia, the report said.

Advertisement

The plan is among several options being drafted by the White House, in a paper aimed at providing alternatives to British Prime Minister Keir Starmer ceding sovereignty of the Indian Ocean archipelago to Mauritius, the report said.

“President Trump has been consistent in his position that the United Kingdom should not give away the British Indian Ocean Territory, which includes our joint U.S.-UK military facility on the Diego Garcia atoll,” a U.S. official told Reuters.

“Diego Garcia’s strategic location in the Indian Ocean makes it a vital and indispensable military installation of significant importance to the national security of the United States,” the official said, adding the U.S. remains in regular discussions with Britain to preserve the island’s viability as a regional security platform.

Advertisement

Britain’s government in April put on hold its deal to cede sovereignty of the Chagos Islands, home to the U.S.-British Diego Garcia air base, which had been criticized by U.S. President Donald Trump.

Trump said in February that the deal was a “big mistake.”

“The government inherited a situation where UK control over the military base on Diego Garcia was under threat and action was required to protect UK national interests and to prevent our adversaries from getting a foothold in a location of such strategic importance,” a UK government spokesperson said.

“Diego Garcia is a key strategic military asset for both the UK and the U.S., which has protected our shared security for nearly 60 years,” the spokesperson added. “Maintaining long-term operational control and security of Diego Garcia is the entire basis for the UK-Mauritius agreement, an agreement borne out of the real long-term risks to the base of which both the UK and U.S. are very well aware.”

Advertisement

(Reporting by Gnaneshwar Rajan and Rhea Rose Abraham in Bengaluru and Bo Erickson in Washington; Editing by Chris Reese)

Related
White HouseChagos Islands

articles.title: British Deputy Prime Minister Tells JD Vance He Was Wrong To Blame Immigration For Teen's Murder
articles.content:
伦敦（美联社）——英国副首相周日表示，他已告诉美国副总统万斯，称其将一名大学生遭刺伤后被铐住双手、躺在血泊中死亡的责任归咎于移民是错误的。

身兼司法大臣的大卫·拉米表示，他在周六与万斯进行了一次他形容为“激烈”的电话交谈中挑战了对方。拉米和万斯尽管政治立场不同，但因宗教信仰和家庭背景相似而建立了友谊。

“我们进行了一次愉快的对话，因为我们有良好的关系，但我希望让他清楚，我不同意他提出的一些事实，并向他们呈现事实，”拉米在接受天空新闻采访时表示。

这通电话发生在万斯在社交平台X上发帖称对18岁学生亨利·诺瓦克的遇害应抱有“正当愤怒”之后一天。诺瓦克于去年12月在英国南安普敦被维克拉姆·迪格瓦刺伤后死亡。

迪格瓦是锡克教徒，他向警方虚假声称自己是种族主义袭击的受害者，而诺瓦克是白人。当警察到达时，他们最初将这名受伤男子视为嫌疑人，后来才发现他的伤势并试图进行心肺复苏。

万斯似乎将这起谋杀部分归咎于“大量移民涌入，其中许多人对西方及其热爱者怀有敌意。”

拉米表示，他希望向万斯“强调几件事”，包括凶手是英国人，目前已被关押。

“这与大规模移民毫无关系，”拉米说。

23岁的迪格瓦用一把8英寸（21厘米）的锡克教匕首刺死诺瓦克，本周被判定谋杀罪名成立，被判处终身监禁，最少服刑21年。

该案件被英国反移民活动人士和政客利用。周二，在南安普敦举行的追悼诺瓦克示威活动中，警察遭到椅子、罐头、石头和照明弹的袭击，出席者包括极右翼人士。

首相斯塔默办公室周五针对万斯的言论发表声明，批评那些“试图干涉我们的民主并试图在我们的街头煽动分裂”的人。

负责调查警察不当行为的警察行为独立办公室正在调查现场警察的行为。

受害者父亲马克·诺瓦克曾表示，此案与种族主义或宗教无关，他希望儿子的死能带来更安全的街道，而不是被用来制造“进一步的分裂、仇恨或紧张局势”。

拉米还表示，他告诉万斯，“以这种方式发推特没有帮助，部分原因是诺瓦克一家提出的请求，并提醒他注意他们希望不要将这变成一个分裂和仇恨的问题，而是要将其变成一个常识问题。”
articles.summary:
LOADINGERROR LOADING
&

LONDON (AP) — Britain’s deputy prime minister said Sunday that he told U.S. Vice President JD Vance he was wrong to blame immigration for the death of a university student who was handcuffed as he lay dying from a stab wound.

David Lammy, who is also the justice minister, said he challenged Vance in what he described as a “robust” phone call on Saturday. Lammy and Vance have struck up a friendship, based on their religious beliefs and family backgrounds, even though they come from different sides of the political spectrum.

Advertisement

“We had an agreeable conversation because we have got a relationship, but I wanted to make him clear that I disagree with some of the facts that he was asserting and to present the facts to him,” Lammy told Sky News.

The call came a day after Vance said in a post on social platform X that there should be “righteous anger” in response to the murder of Henry Nowak, 18, who died in December after being stabbed by Vickrum Digwa in the English city of Southampton.

Digwa, who is Sikh, falsely claimed to police he was the victim of a racist assault by Nowak, who was white. When police officers arrived, they initially treated the wounded man as a suspect before noticing his injury and trying to resuscitate him.

Advertisement

Vance appeared to blame the murder in part on “the mass invasion of migrants, many of whom despise the West and the people who love it.”

Lammy said he wanted to “emphasize a number of things” to Vance, including that the killer was British and is now behind bars.

“This has got nothing to do with mass migration,” Lammy said.

Advertisement

Digwa, 23, was convicted of murder for stabbing Nowak with an 8-inch (21 centimeter) Sikh dagger and sentenced this week to life in prison with a minimum 21-year term.

The case has been seized on by anti-immigration activists and politicians in the U.K. On Tuesday, police in Southampton were pelted with chairs, cans, rocks and flares after a demonstration over Nowak’s death attended by far-right figures and others.

In a statement issued Friday in response to Vance’s comments, Prime Minister Keir Starmer ’s office criticized people “trying to interfere in our democracy and seeking to stir up division on our streets.”

Advertisement

The Independent Office for Police Conduct, which investigates allegations of police wrongdoing, is probing the actions of police officers on the scene.

The victim’s father, Mark Nowak, has said the case was not about racism or religion, and that he wanted his son’s death to lead to safer streets and not to be used to create “further division, hatred or tension.”

Lammy also said he told Vance “it’s not helpful to tweet in this way, partly because of what the Nowak family have asked for, and reminded him about their desire not to make this an issue of division and hatred, but to make this an issue of common sense.”
